meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with a professional SaaS starter kit. Learn to build your first portfolio-ready app using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at blank IDEs. The most effective way to overcome project paralysis is leveraging a production-grade SaaS starter kit. By combining Antigravity's rapid scaffolding with the reasoning power of Claude Code, you transform abstract ideas into functional, deployable software, ensuring your first project is resume-worthy and technically robust.
content.intro:
The screen glows in the dark, a silent testament to the 'tutorial hell' you've been trapped in for months. You know the syntax, you understand the theory, but every time you open a blank repository, the sheer weight of architectural decisions brings your progress to a grinding halt. You aren't failing because you lack skill; you're failing because you're trying to build the engine from scratch before you've even designed the chassis.

Professional developers don't reinvent the wheel; we stand on the shoulders of optimized boilerplate. By shifting your focus from 'writing code' to 'orchestrating features,' you reclaim your momentum. This guide strips away the friction of setup, allowing you to build, ship, and iterate on a real-world SaaS product that proves your technical competency to future employers.

steps.title: Initialize the Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish a production-ready foundation using Antigravity to ensure your project includes authentication, database integration, and UI components from second one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS starter template using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind. Include user authentication, subscription billing structure, and a protected dashboard route.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to translate your high-level project idea into concrete database schemas and API endpoints, preventing scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on my project idea [INSERT IDEA], create a normalized schema for the database and map the necessary API endpoints to handle CRUD operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'analyze the project requirements and generate the Prisma schema for the database models'

steps.title: Implement Core Features
steps.description: Focus on the unique value proposition of your app while letting the starter kit handle the boilerplate user management and security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement the core feature logic for [FEATURE NAME]. Ensure it follows secure coding practices and integrates with the existing authentication context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'write the server-side logic for the core feature, ensuring it is modular and unit tested'

steps.title: Build the Responsive UI
steps.description: Leverage existing design patterns within your starter kit to build a high-fidelity interface without spending weeks on CSS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive dashboard interface using the existing component library that displays the data from the core feature implemented in Step 3.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'create the dashboard page component following the existing design system'

steps.title: Automate Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline so that every push is tested and deployed, mimicking professional team workflows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'generate a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for automated testing and production deployment'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Time to First Commit' over 'Perfect Architecture'; you can refactor later, but you can't refactor a project that doesn't exist.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a black box; learn how to use it before you try to rewrite its internal core libraries.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; this builds a portfolio that is actually readable by other senior engineers.
Limit your MVP to exactly one core feature; students often fail by trying to build a 'platform' instead of a 'tool'.
Deploy your staging environment on day one; seeing your code live on a public URL drastically increases your motivation to finish.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, document your learning process in a README.md file and share your live project link on LinkedIn to start networking with potential employers.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the expertise to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day your portfolio remains empty. Stop overthinking the stack and start demonstrating your value to the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
